title: 48% of artists use AI to make music - fewer than in 2023
text:
48% of artists use AI to make music - fewer than in 2023
Fewer real musicians are using AI to create music compared to 2023.
May 20, 2025
A new artist survey carried out by global music distribution company Ditto Music has found that more artists currently use AI in their creative process than in 2023. ​
The follow-up to
Ditto's 2023 survey
shows that 48% of artists have used AI in some aspect of their music-making, down from 59.5% in 2023. This includes tasks such as songwriting, artwork creation, mastering, and general music production.
These findings come shortly after reports of
20,000 bot-generated tracks are being uploaded to streaming services every day,
and
10% of consumers saying they use generative AI to create music
.
Ditto's survey also found that the main reason artists are steering away from AI in 2025 is the lack of personal creativity it offers, with 42% of respondents citing this as their biggest concern - rising from 23% just two years ago.
“It’s great to see that even with how accessible AI has become, so many artists are sticking to their own creative instincts. AI can be a helpful tool, especially when you hit a creative block, but at the end of the day, it’s the unique voices and visions of our artist community that really makes all the difference."
​
​
​
Lee Parsons, Ditto Music CEO.
Interestingly, of those who currently use AI in their music, only 18% had been using it in 2023, indicating significant growth in AI adoption over the past two years.
Still, despite this increased usage, just 18% of artists now say they would never use AI in their music, down from 29% in 2023, suggesting a growing openness as the technology becomes more accessible.

here
